# streamkap_kafka_user (Resource)

Manages a **Streamkap Kafka user** with ACL rules for topic access control.

This resource creates and manages Kafka users that can connect to the Streamkap Kafka proxy endpoint with fine-grained access control via ACL rules.

[Documentation](https://docs.streamkap.com/streamkap-provider-for-terraform)



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password for the Kafka user. Must be 12-128 characters.

**Security:** This value is marked sensitive and will not appear in CLI output or logs. Write-only: not returned by the API on read.
- `username` (String) Username for the Kafka user. Must be 1-24 characters, alphanumeric with dashes only (cannot start or end with a dash). **Cannot be changed after creation.**

### Optional

- `is_create_schema_registry` (Boolean) Whether to create a schema registry user alongside the Kafka user. Defaults to `false`.
- `kafka_acls` (Block List) List of Kafka ACL rules for this user. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--kafka_acls))
- `whitelist_ips` (String) Comma-separated list of whitelisted IP addresses or CIDR ranges. Maximum 1000 characters.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the Kafka user. Same as the username.
- `kafka_proxy_endpoint` (String) The Kafka proxy endpoint for this user. Computed by the server.
- `schema_proxy_endpoint` (String) The schema registry proxy endpoint for this user. Computed by the server.

<a id="nestedblock--kafka_acls"></a>
### Nested Schema for `kafka_acls`

Required:

- `operation` (String) The ACL operation. Valid values for TOPIC resource: `ALL`, `ALTER`, `ALTER_CONFIGS`, `CREATE`, `DELETE`, `DESCRIBE`, `DESCRIBE_CONFIGS`, `READ`, `WRITE`. Valid values for GROUP resource: `DELETE`, `DESCRIBE`, `READ`.
- `resource_pattern_type` (String) The resource pattern type. Valid values: `LITERAL`, `PREFIXED`.
- `topic_name` (String) The topic name or pattern for this ACL rule.

Optional:

- `resource` (String) The resource type. Valid values: `TOPIC`, `GROUP`. Defaults to `TOPIC`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import streamkap_kafka_user.example my-kafka-user
```
